Home.heroEyebrow: General Authority of the National Library and Archives of Egypt
Home.heroSubtitle: A single destination for the National Library, Archives, and Museum — bringing three millennia of written and cultural heritage within reach.
Home.heroImageAlt: The golden funerary mask of Tutankhamun, Egyptian Museum
Home.ctaBandText: Join as a member to unlock the Digital Reading Room and support our preservation work.

library.title: National Library
library.subtitle: Three thousand years of the written word
library.description: Browse a growing catalog of manuscripts, printed books, periodicals, and maps spanning Egypt's literary and scholarly history.

archives.title: National Archives
archives.subtitle: The paper trail of a nation
archives.description: Explore administrative records, correspondence, engineering plans, and photographs documenting modern Egyptian history.

museum.title: Digital Museum
museum.subtitle: Masterworks across the ages
museum.description: Discover high-resolution imagery and 3D models of artifacts spanning Pharaonic, Islamic, and modern Egypt.

collections.title: Digital Collections
collections.subtitle: Stories that cross the archive
collections.description: Curated, cross-module collections weaving together books, records, and objects around a single theme.

exhibitions.title: Virtual Exhibitions
exhibitions.subtitle: Immersive storytelling
exhibitions.description: Step into scroll-driven exhibitions that bring artifacts and history to life.

timelines.title: Interactive Timelines
timelines.subtitle: Five thousand years, one thread
timelines.description: Navigate the eras of Egyptian history through an interactive, artifact-linked timeline.

restorationLab.title: Restoration Laboratory
restorationLab.subtitle: Behind the conservation bench
restorationLab.description: See how our conservators bring fragile manuscripts and artifacts back to life.

research.title: Research Portal
research.subtitle: Tools built for scholars
research.description: Advanced search, citation exports, and material request workflows for academic researchers.

learn.title: Educational Platform
learn.subtitle: Learning paths for every curiosity
learn.description: Structured lessons on Egyptology, conservation, and heritage studies for all levels.

events.title: Events Portal
events.subtitle: What's happening
events.description: Lectures, workshops, tours, and open days across the Authority's venues.

assistant.title: AI Heritage Assistant
assistant.subtitle: Ask anything about Egypt's heritage
assistant.description: A conversational guide to the Library, Archives, and Museum's collections.

membership.title: Membership
membership.subtitle: Join the community of stewards
membership.description: Tiered membership plans unlocking research access and exclusive events.

readingRoom.title: Digital Reading Room
readingRoom.subtitle: Secure access to rare materials
readingRoom.description: A controlled-access viewer for restricted archival and library materials.

publications.title: Publications
publications.subtitle: Scholarship in print
publications.description: Journals, monographs, and reference guides published by the Authority.

about.title: About the Authority
about.intro: The General Authority of the National Library and Archives of Egypt safeguards and shares the nation's written and documentary heritage.

visit.title: Plan Your Visit
visit.intro: Everything you need to know before visiting our reading rooms, galleries, and research facilities.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro: Reach our teams for research inquiries, partnerships, or general questions.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.intro: Our commitment to an inclusive, accessible experience for every visitor, in line with WCAG 2.2 AA.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: How we collect, use, and protect your information across our digital services.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: The terms governing use of the EgyptVerse Platform.

sitemap.title: Sitemap
sitemap.intro: A complete overview of every section of the platform.

news.title: News
news.intro: Announcements, exhibition openings, and stories from across the Authority.

Membership.title: Membership Plans
Membership.subtitle: Choose the plan that fits how you engage with Egypt's heritage
Membership.accountNotice: This account view uses a mocked, signed-in member for Phase 1 demonstration purposes only — there is no real authentication yet.

ReadingRoom.gateDescription: Access to restricted archival and library materials is granted to verified researchers and members.
ReadingRoom.sessionNotice: This is a controlled-access viewer. Downloading and printing are disabled for restricted materials.

Assistant.welcomeMessage: Ahlan! I'm the Heritage Assistant. Ask me anything about the Library, Archives, or Museum collections.
Assistant.inputPlaceholder: Ask about a manuscript, artifact, or era…
Assistant.disclaimer: Responses are illustrative and drawn from mock data in this Phase 1 preview.
Assistant.replyRosetta: The Rosetta Stone decipherment is documented in our Library's Egyptology collection — see Champollion's decipherment journal, and its story is retold on the Modern Egypt timeline.
Assistant.replyMask: The Funerary Mask of a Priestess is on display in the Digital Museum's Pharaonic Egypt gallery, from the 21st Dynasty.
Assistant.replyMembership: You can explore our three membership tiers — Explorer, Researcher, and Patron — on the Membership page under Access & Membership.
Assistant.replyDefault: That's a great question for our collections. Try exploring the National Library, Digital Museum, or Interactive Timelines — this Phase 1 preview uses illustrative mock answers rather than a live model.

Research.requestsIntro: Submit a request to view restricted materials or export citations for your research.

Manuscripts.title: Manuscripts of Egypt
Manuscripts.subtitle: Five scripts, one civilization
Manuscripts.description: From hieroglyphic-adjacent Ptolemaic decrees to Ottoman-era Qur'ans, Egypt's manuscript heritage spans five great writing traditions layered across three thousand years. This is our most ambitious digitization initiative yet.
Manuscripts.gridDescription: Each tradition carries its own script, material, and craft — together they trace an unbroken line of Egyptian literacy from the Ptolemies to the Ottomans.
